story.title: Dr. Sarah Chen and the System of Healing
story.text:
I am Dr. Sarah Chen. I am a research scientist. I study how the body repairs itself. It is an amazing and complex system. Think about a simple paper cut. Your body knows what to do instantly.

The Wound Severity is the first factor. A deep cut sends a big signal for help. This signal increases the Cell Division Rate. Cells near the cut start dividing quickly. Good Blood Supply is also very important. Blood delivers oxygen and nutrients to these busy cells. This helps them divide even faster.

A high Cell Division Rate speeds up New Tissue Formation. Your body builds new skin and blood vessels. As this new tissue grows, we see Healing Progress. The cut gets smaller and closes. However, a high Wound Severity slows overall healing. A bigger wound needs more time to repair. My team and I create computer models of this system. We hope to find new ways to help people heal.
think_abouts: What might happen to healing if a person has poor blood supply?
Why does a small paper cut heal faster than a deep scrape?
story.go_deeper: How could a scientist like Dr. Chen use a computer model to test a new medicine for healing?
check_prompts: What is one factor that increases the Cell Division Rate?

vocabulary.definition: How deep and large a cut is; a bigger wound has higher severity.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The doctor checked the wound severity to see if it needed stitches.

vocabulary.definition: The flow of blood that brings oxygen and nutrients to an area.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Good blood supply is important for healthy skin.

vocabulary.definition: How fast cells make copies of themselves to create new tissue.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The cell division rate increases when your body needs to repair an injury.

vocabulary.definition: The process of growing new skin and other tissues to close a wound.
vocabulary.use_sentence: A scab protects the area of new tissue formation.

vocabulary.definition: How well a wound is closing and repairing over time.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The nurse checked the healing progress of the patient's cut each day.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Chen calls healing a 'complex system.' What other systems in your daily life have many parts that work together?
discussion_cards.frame: One complex system I can think of is ___, because it has parts like ___ and ___ that work together to ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Based on the reading, what is one positive relationship and one negative relationship in the healing system? Explain why.
discussion_cards.frame: A positive relationship is between ___ and ___, because ___. A negative relationship is between ___ and ___, because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Imagine you could control one part of the healing system to make a cut heal faster. Which part would you choose and why?
discussion_cards.frame: To make a cut heal faster, I would increase the ___ because ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
